query_log.query: 2026-03-23 Reuters 米株引け 米10年 ドル円 原油 日経
2026-03-23 TDnet シンカ フリークHD 資本業務提携 業績予想修正
2026-03-23 Reuters KADOKAWA Oasis 明海グループ ホルムズ

snapshot: Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 51,060, down 1,930 versus Osaka day session. The cash Nikkei opened at 52,468.72 and by 10:00 JST had fallen to 50,820.91.
At 10:00 JST, the Prime market had 39 gainers versus 1,530 losers. Only three Nikkei 225 names were up, underscoring the breadth of the selloff.
U.S. equities fell again on March 20. The S&P 500 closed at 6,506.48 (-1.51%), the Nasdaq at 21,647.61 (-2.01%), and the Dow at 45,577.47 (-0.96%).
USD/JPY traded in the upper 159s while the U.S. 10-year yield reached 4.4110%. Intervention risk near 160 remains, but yen-weakness pressure is still strong.
Brent was around $111.90 and WTI around $98.35. Even domestic defensives are struggling, leaving relative strength concentrated in stock-specific stories.

drivers.driver: A break below 53,000 raises forced-selling risk
drivers.why_today: In Reuters' strategist comment, a deeper break below 53,000 in the Nikkei was described as a level that could trigger margin-call selling, with 50,000 cited as a downside guide.

drivers.driver: A war-risk regime of oil, yields and a weaker yen
drivers.why_today: Higher oil and higher U.S. yields are driving a global risk-off move. Yen weakness remains, but cost pressure is making it hard for broad domestic defensives to work.

drivers.driver: Activist, tie-up and earnings-revision stories are the only clean relative strength
drivers.why_today: Capital is concentrating in names with explainable stock-specific catalysts, including KADOKAWA on Oasis, Shinka on the SB C&S tie-up, and FreakOut on an earnings upgrade.

headline_inventory.headline: Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 51,060, down 1,930 versus the Osaka day session
Asian equities slumped while the U.S. 10-year hit 4.4110%, USD/JPY traded around 159.15, and Brent stayed near $111.90
The Nikkei opened at 52,468.72 and later extended losses to more than 2,000 points
MUFG eSmart Securities said a deeper move below 53,000 could shift the Nikkei's downside guide toward 50,000
USD/JPY traded in the low 159s, while Japan's top FX official said the government was ready to respond 'on all fronts'
KADOKAWA rose against the tape after Oasis was found to hold 8.86%, making it the top Prime-market gainer at 9:45 JST
Shinka disclosed a capital and business alliance with SB C&S, adding an on-market share-purchase element to the partnership
Meikai Group surged on tanker-market strength, with some traders pointing to money rotating away from Kyoei Tanker after tighter credit rules
FreakOut revised FY September 2026 guidance upward after announcing the sale of investment securities
Katakura & Co-op Agri attracted renewed fertilizer-theme buying on longer-lasting Hormuz risk

watchlist.name: KADOKAWA
watchlist.why_today: Oasis was disclosed as an 8.86% holder. At 9:45 JST the stock was the top Prime-market gainer, clearly outperforming a collapsing tape.
watchpoints: Can it stay near the top of Prime gainers through the late morning session
Can it hold around the 3,300 yen area on real dip-buying
Does the move remain stock-specific rather than sector-led
falsifiers: It fails to hold positive territory and the move fades into a short-lived news trade
The market treats the Oasis stake as passive and stops pricing in change
watchlist.red_team: No concrete demand has been disclosed yet, so the move can still fade after the headline shock.

sources.title: ホットストック：ＫＡＤＯＫＡＷＡが逆行高、オアシスが大株主に
カドカワは逆行高、オアシスが大量保有

watchlist.name: Shinka
watchlist.why_today: The SB C&S capital and business alliance is still drawing follow-through buying. It remained among the top gainers in the 10:21 update.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 1,000 yen area and stay high in the gainers ranking
Does turnover remain active enough to support continuation buying
Does the SB C&S distribution angle retain market interest
falsifiers: Profit-taking from the earlier surge takes over
The tie-up is treated as a theme without visible earnings impact
watchlist.red_team: The strategic direction is attractive, but near-term earnings visibility is still limited.

sources.title: シンカ(149A) SB C&S株式会社との資本業務提携に関するお知らせ
株価値上がりランキング

watchlist.name: Meikai Group
watchlist.why_today: The stock is jumping on tanker-rate strength, with traders also pointing to short-term money rotating away from Kyoei Tanker after tighter credit rules.
watchpoints: Can it hold a double-digit percentage gain
Does the rotation from Kyoei Tanker keep flowing into Meikai
Does buying stay concentrated in the stock rather than the whole shipping group
falsifiers: A calmer oil or war headline strips away the theme bid
The move loses momentum quickly after the opening surge
watchlist.red_team: This is a theme-led move, so the life of the trade can be short.

sources.title: 明海グループが大幅高で５日連続の大陽線、タンカー関連株への投資マネー攻勢続く◇
株価値上がりランキング

watchlist.name: FreakOut Holdings
watchlist.why_today: The FY upgrade tied to the investment-security sale is a clear numeric catalyst. The stock is still positive despite the broad market selloff.
watchpoints: Can it keep climbing the gainers list
Does the market focus on the improved FY numbers rather than the one-off element
Can it absorb selling pressure around the 700 yen area
falsifiers: The move is dismissed as a special-factor gain
Weakness in small-cap growth names overwhelms the stock-specific story
watchlist.red_team: If investors question the quality of the upgrade, follow-through buying may stay limited.

sources.title: フリークＨＤ(6094) 投資有価証券の売却及び業績予想の修正に関するお知らせ
フリークＨＤ---急騰、投資有価証券の売却及び業績予想の修正を発表

watchlist.name: Katakura & Co-op Agri
watchlist.why_today: Longer-lasting Hormuz risk is reviving fertilizer-theme buying. The stock stayed high in the gainers ranking in the 10:21 update.
watchpoints: Can Katakura remain the lead vehicle within the fertilizer theme
Does sympathy buying continue into Taki Chemical and OAT Agrio
Does the oil and transport-cost narrative stay intact
falsifiers: Theme buying is exhausted before the close
The market decides the move is purely speculative and reverses it sharply
watchlist.red_team: This is still a theme trade ahead of clear demand confirmation, so reversals can be fast.

sources.title: 片倉コープが急騰、中東混迷長期化への懸念で肥料関連に思惑的な買い入る◇
株価値上がりランキング

deep_dive.title: KADOKAWA: In a market washout, test whether the activist surprise can keep leading

lines: When more than 90% of the market is falling, simple and forceful catalysts such as capital-structure change or a new activist holder can matter more than ordinary earnings narratives.
KADOKAWA has enough liquidity and a clear re-rating angle through the Oasis stake disclosure.
Reuters reported that at 9:45 JST KADOKAWA was the top Prime-market gainer, with Oasis' 8.86% holding treated as the catalyst.
The broader tape is weak enough that it is easier than usual to tell whether this is genuine relative strength or just a brief headline spike.
Can it hold around the 3,300 yen area instead of fading after the opening burst.
Does volume remain concentrated in the stock itself rather than spread across the sector.
The stock fails to stay positive and the move fades into a short-lived news trade.
The market treats the Oasis stake as passive and stops extending the change narrative.
The content of any future request or engagement is still unclear, so expectations are running ahead of specifics.
If the market selloff deepens further, even relative-strength names can be hit by profit-taking.

sources.title: ホットストック：ＫＡＤＯＫＡＷＡが逆行高、オアシスが大株主に
カドカワは逆行高、オアシスが大量保有
日経平均は続落で寄り付く、連休中の米株安で　足元は2000円超安

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei breaks materially below 53,000 and forced selling becomes the dominant tape
If oil or Hormuz headlines reverse sharply and break the thematic premise for Meikai Group or Katakura & Co-op Agri
If KADOKAWA or Shinka fail to hold positive territory and their relative-strength case disappears
If official rhetoric causes a sharp FX reversal and resets the market's relative-strength map
content.disclaimer: This is a morning market digest, not investment advice. Please make your own final decisions.
